name: Enchilada Pie
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine beef, onion, and garlic powder in a large skillet. Cook over medium heat until meat is browned, stirring to crumble. Drain well.
2. Add the remaining seven ingredients (tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, taco seasoning mix, salt, pepper, chili powder, cream of chicken soup, and milk) to the skillet. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
3. Tear each corn tortilla into 8 pieces. Place half of the tortilla pieces in a 9 x 13-inch pan, top with the meat mixture.
4. Arrange the remaining tortilla pieces evenly over the meat mixture.
5. Pour the soup and milk mixture over the tortillas.
6. Sprinkle cheese evenly over the top.
7. Bake at 350°F for 45 minutes. The original recipe said it made 6 servings, but I thought more like 10. It would easily make two square foil pans to feed four people each (generously). Do not bake before freezing. Serve with corn and a green salad. True, the corn tortillas did get a bit mushy, but the flavor was good and reminded me of "real" tamales.
